hook.text: Surveying every household in a country is impossible. What if you sampled entire neighborhoods instead?

buildup.text: Cluster sampling picks random groups — schools, clinics, cities — and surveys everyone in them.

discovery.text: It's cheaper because you visit fewer locations. One trip to a village covers dozens of respondents.

twist.text: Clusters may not be representative. A wealthy neighborhood doesn't reflect the whole city.

climax.text: The WHO uses cluster sampling to estimate disease rates in countries with no census data.

punchline.text: Can't reach everyone? Sample in clusters.

quiz.question: Why is cluster sampling used?
options: It's cheaper — you survey entire natural groups instead of scattered individuals
It's more accurate than random sampling
